title: Accessibility audit (axe-core)
description:
# Accessibility Audit (axe-core) ## Purpose Ensure mockups meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards. Zero critical violations required. ## Tools - **axe-core**: Industry-standard accessibility testing engine - **@axe-core/playwright**: Integration with Playwright tests - **axe DevTools**: Browser extension for manual testing ## Automated Testing ### Install ```bash npm install -D @axe-core/playwright ``` ### Test Integration ```javascript // tests/accessibility.spec.js import { test, expect } from "@playwright/test"; import AxeBuilder from "@axe-core/playwright"; const pages = [ "/dist/admin/index.html", "/dist/admin/organizations/index.html", "/dist/admin/organizations/detail.html", "/dist/portal/index.html", "/dist/portal/licenses/index.html", "/dist/portal/seats/assign.html" ]; for (const page of pages) { test(`No critical a11y violations: ${page}`, async ({ page: pw }) => { await pw.goto(page); const results = await new AxeBuilder({ page: pw }) .withTags(["wcag2a", "wcag2aa", "wcag21aa"]) .analyze(); const critical = results.violations.filter(v => v.impact === "critical"); const serious = results.violations.filter(v => v.impact === "serious"); expect(critical).toHaveLength(0); expect(serious).toHaveLength(0); // Log moderate/minor for review const other = results.violations.filter(v => v.impact === "moderate" || v.impact === "minor" ); if (other.length > 0) { console.log(`${page}: ${other.length} minor/moderate issues`); } }); } ``` ## Common Issues to Fix ### 1. Missing Form Labels ```html ``` ### 2. Missing Button Labels (Icon-Only) ```html ``` ### 3. Color Contrast Minimum contrast ratios: - Normal text: 4.5:1 - Large text (18px+): 3:1 - UI components: 3:1 ```css /* Check these combinations */ .text-gray-500 /* on white: may fail */ .text-gray-600 /* on white: usually passes */ ``` ### 4. Focus Indicators ```css /* Ensure visible focus */ :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #482d8b; outline-offset: 2px; } /* Never remove focus without replacement */ /* Bad: */ :focus { outline: none; } ``` ### 5. Heading Hierarchy ```html

Dashboard

Organizations

Dashboard

Organizations

``` ### 6. ARIA on Interactive Elements ```html

Create Organization

Content
``` ### 7. Live Regions for Dynamic Content ```html
Organization created successfully
Loading organizations...
``` ## Manual Testing Checklist ### Keyboard Navigation - [ ] All interactive elements reachable via Tab - [ ] Focus order matches visual order - [ ] Escape closes modals/dropdowns - [ ] Enter/Space activates buttons - [ ] Arrow keys navigate menus ### Screen Reader Testing Use VoiceOver (Mac) or NVDA (Windows): - [ ] Page title announced on load - [ ] Headings structure makes sense - [ ] Form fields announce labels - [ ] Buttons announce purpose - [ ] Tables announce row/column context - [ ] Dynamic changes announced ### Visual Testing - [ ] Zoom to 200% — layout still usable - [ ] High contrast mode — content visible - [ ] Reduced motion — no essential animations ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] axe-core: 0 critical violations - [ ] axe-core: 0 serious violations - [ ] All pages keyboard navigable - [ ] All forms have associated labels - [ ] All icon buttons have aria-label - [ ] Color contrast meets 4.5:1 - [ ] Focus indicators visible - [ ] Heading hierarchy correct - [ ] Screen reader testing passes

title: Build School-to-Home portal configuration
description:
# Build School-to-Home Portal Configuration

Create the School-to-Home setup and management pages. This feature is only available for Premium licenses.

## Files to Create

- `portal/school-to-home/index.html` - All portals overview
- `portal/school-to-home/portal-detail.html` - Single portal config
- `portal/school-to-home/setup.html` - New portal setup wizard
- `portal/school-to-home/branding.html` - Logo/branding settings

## Overview Page (index.html)

**For Premium licenses:** Show portal cards with URL, status, classroom count, last updated

**For Base licenses:** Show upgrade prompt with feature benefits

## Portal Detail Page

**Settings:**
- Custom URL slug (preview full URL)
- Access password (show/hide, copy, change)
- Logo upload with preview
- Portal status toggle (active/paused)

**Classrooms section:** Table linking to classroom management

## Setup Wizard (setup.html)

**Step 1:** Choose URL slug, validate availability
**Step 2:** Set access password
**Step 3:** Upload logo (optional)
**Step 4:** Confirmation with portal URL

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Premium license shows portal management
- [ ] Base license shows upgrade CTA
- [ ] Portal detail shows all settings
- [ ] URL slug preview updates live
- [ ] Password show/hide toggle works
- [ ] Logo upload shows preview
- [ ] Status toggle updates immediately
- [ ] Setup wizard completes all 4 steps

## Verification

1. Load index.html with Premium license - portals visible
2. Load index.html with Base license - upgrade prompt shown
3. Edit portal settings - changes reflect in UI
4. Complete setup wizard - success state shows portal URL

title: Mobile responsive testing
description: Test all pages at: 375px (mobile), 640px (sm), 768px (md), 1024px (lg), 1280px (xl). Verify: 3-column collapse, sidebar drawer, command palette full-screen, table horizontal scroll, tab→accordion conversion.
acceptance_criteria: Layout works at xl (1280px+), lg (1024px), md (768px), sm (640px). 3-column layout collapses on smaller screens. Sidebar toggles on mobile. Tables scroll horizontally. Touch targets are 44px minimum.

title: Add form validation and error states
description: Add validation to all forms: inline field errors, error summary at top, focus first error on submit. Add error states for: network failure (retry button), empty search, no permissions, 404 pages.
acceptance_criteria: Required fields show validation on blur. Error messages appear below invalid fields. Form cannot submit with validation errors. Success state shows after valid submission.

title: Test task - delete me
description: Testing database write operations

title: Complete all modal interactions
description: Verify all modals: Create Organization, Edit Organization, Create License Pool, Invite User, Assign Seat, Create Classroom, Confirm dialogs. Test: open, interact, close, focus trap, escape key, focus restoration.
acceptance_criteria: All modals open and close correctly. Modal backdrop closes on click. Escape key closes modal. Focus traps inside modal. First focusable element receives focus on open.

title: Add empty and loading states
description: Add skeleton loading screens (300ms minimum display). Add empty states with CTAs: no organizations, no licenses, no users, no search results. Verify aria-busy on loading containers.
acceptance_criteria: Empty state shows for tables with no data. Loading skeleton shows while data loads. Error state shows with retry option. All states match design system colors.

title: Fix all broken links (link integrity)
description: Run: npx linkinator ./dist --recurse. Fix all broken internal links. Verify navigation between all pages works. Test: dashboard → list → detail → back flows for all entities.
acceptance_criteria: linkinator reports zero broken links. All internal navigation links work. External links open in new tab. Hash links scroll to correct section.

title: Phase 5: Organization Portal Extended
description:
# Phase 5: Organization Portal Extended

## Purpose

Complete the Organization Portal with advanced features:

1. **Roster Management** — User lifecycle (invite, manage, bulk import)
2. **School-to-Home** — Parent portal configuration (Premium feature)
3. **Classroom Management** — Organize students for School-to-Home
4. **Settings** — Organization profile and admin management

---

## Pages to Build

### 1. Roster Management (`portal/roster/`)

#### Roster List (`index.html`)

**Purpose**: View and manage all users in the organization

**Stats Row**:
- Total Users
- Active Users
- Pending Invitations
- Seats Assigned

**User Table**:
| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Checkbox | Bulk selection |
| Name | First + Last name |
| Email | Email address |
| Role | Owner, Admin, Member (badges) |
| Seats | Count of assigned seats (hover shows which) |
| Status | Active, Pending, Inactive (badge) |
| Last Active | Timestamp or "Never" |
| Actions | Edit, Assign Seat, Resend Invite, Remove |

**Filters**:
- Role: All, Owner, Admin, Member
- Status: All, Active, Pending, Inactive
- Seats: All, Has Seat, No Seat
- Search: Name or email

**Bulk Actions** (when rows selected):
- Assign Seats
- Change Role
- Resend Invitations
- Remove Users

**Actions**:
- [+ Invite User] button (prominent)
- [Bulk Import] button

#### Invite User Modal/Page (`invite.html`)

**Purpose**: Add a new user to the organization

**Form Fields**:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           Invite a New User                 │
│                                             │
│ Email address *                             │
│ [teacher@school.edu                   ]     │
│                                             │
│ First name                                  │
│ [Jane                                 ]     │
│                                             │
│ Last name                                   │
│ [Doe                                  ]     │
│                                             │
│ Role *                                      │
│ ○ Admin - Can manage licenses and users     │
│ ● Member - Can use assigned licenses        │
│                                             │
│ ☑ Assign a seat immediately                 │
│   └─ Select license: [Premium - 8 avail ▼]  │
│                                             │
│ Personal message (optional)                 │
│ [Welcome to our school's Calico account\!  ]│
│                                             │
│ [Cancel]                         [Send Invite]
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**States**:
1. **Form** — Initial state
2. **Sending** — Loading spinner
3. **Success** — "Invitation sent to jane@school.edu"
4. **Error (Duplicate)** — "This email is already in your organization"

**After Success**:
- Option to invite another
- Option to view roster
- User appears in roster with "Pending" status

#### Bulk Import Flow (`bulk-import.html`)

**Purpose**: Import multiple users via CSV

**Step 1: Upload**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           Bulk Import Users                 │
│                                             │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                                     │    │
│  │    📄 Drag and drop CSV file here   │    │
│  │         or click to browse          │    │
│  │                                     │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                             │
│  Download template: [users_template.csv]    │
│                                             │
│  Requirements:                              │
│  • CSV format, UTF-8 encoding               │
│  • First row must be headers                │
│  • Required column: email                   │
│  • Optional: first_name, last_name, role    │
│  • Maximum 500 rows per import              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Step 2: Preview**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Preview: users_import.csv (47 rows)        │
│                                             │
│  Column Mapping:                            │
│  ┌────────────┬─────────────┐               │
│  │ CSV Column │ Maps To     │               │
│  ├────────────┼─────────────┤               │
│  │ Email      │ email ✓     │               │
│  │ First      │ first_name  │               │
│  │ Last       │ last_name   │               │
│  │ Type       │ [Select...] │               │
│  └────────────┴─────────────┘               │
│                                             │
│  Preview (first 5 rows):                    │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │ teacher1@school.edu | Jane | Doe    │    │
│  │ teacher2@school.edu | John | Smith  │    │
│  │ ...                                 │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                             │
│  [Back]                      [Validate →]   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Step 3: Validate**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Validation Results                         │
│                                             │
│  ✓ 42 rows ready to import                  │
│  ⚠ 3 rows will update existing users        │
│  ✗ 2 rows have errors                       │
│                                             │
│  Errors:                                    │
│  Row 15: Invalid email format               │
│  Row 23: Duplicate email in file            │
│                                             │
│  [Download Error Report]                    │
│                                             │
│  ☑ Skip rows with errors and continue       │
│                                             │
│  [Back]                      [Import 45 →]  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Step 4: Import Progress**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Importing users...                         │
│                                             │
│  ████████████████████░░░░░░░░ 67%           │
│  30 of 45 processed                         │
│                                             │
│  Please don't close this window.           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Step 5: Summary**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ✓ Import Complete\!                         │
│                                             │
│  42 users created                           │
│  3 users updated                            │
│  0 errors                                   │
│                                             │
│  Invitations will be sent shortly.          │
│                                             │
│  [View Roster]    [Import More]             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Pending Invitations (`pending.html`)

**Purpose**: Manage outstanding invitations

**Table**:
| Email | Invited | Expires | Status | Actions |
|-------|---------|---------|--------|---------|
| jane@... | Mar 1 | Mar 8 | Pending | Resend, Cancel |

**Bulk Actions**:
- Resend All
- Cancel Selected

#### User Detail (`user-detail.html`)

**Purpose**: View and edit a single user within org context

**Content**:
- User info (name, email, phone)
- Role (editable for admins)
- Status
- Seat assignments (add/remove)
- Activity history
- Actions: Edit, Assign Seat, Remove

---

### 2. School-to-Home (`portal/school-to-home/`)

**Feature Context**: School-to-Home is a Premium feature that allows schools to create a parent portal where families can access the Calico curriculum at home.

#### School-to-Home Overview (`index.html`)

**Purpose**: List all School-to-Home portals (one per Premium license)

**For Premium Licenses**:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  School-to-Home Portal                      │
│  Premium License - 50 seats                 │
│                                             │
│  Portal URL: calico.es/springfield         │
│  Status: ● Active                           │
│  Classrooms: 12                             │
│  Students: 180                              │
│                                             │
│  Last updated: March 1, 2024                │
│                                             │
│  [Configure Portal]  [View as Parent]       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**For Base Licenses** (Upgrade Prompt):
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🌟 School-to-Home is a Premium Feature     │
│                                             │
│  Give families access to Calico curriculum  │
│  at home with custom portals and passwords. │
│                                             │
│  Benefits:                                  │
│  • Custom branded portal                    │
│  • Classroom organization                   │
│  • Simple parent access codes               │
│  • Track home practice                      │
│                                             │
│  [Upgrade to Premium]                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Portal Configuration (`portal-detail.html`)

**Purpose**: Configure a single School-to-Home portal

**URL Settings**:
- Custom slug: `calico.es/[___________]`
- Preview of full URL
- Availability check (unique slug)

**Access Settings**:
- Access password (show/hide, copy, regenerate)
- Password hint text

**Branding**:
- Logo upload (drag-drop or file picker)
- Logo preview
- Remove logo option

**Portal Status**:
- Toggle: Active / Inactive
- Warning when deactivating

**Classrooms Section**:
- List of all classrooms for this portal
- Columns: Name, Teacher, Grade, Students, Access Code
- Actions: Edit, Delete
- [+ Add Classroom] button

#### Portal Setup Wizard (`setup.html`)

**Purpose**: First-time setup of School-to-Home portal

**Step 1: Welcome**
- Explanation of School-to-Home
- What they'll configure
- [Get Started] button

**Step 2: Configure URL**
- Choose custom slug
- See full URL preview
- Uniqueness validation

**Step 3: Set Password**
- Create access password
- Strength indicator
- Confirm password

**Step 4: Upload Logo (Optional)**
- Drag-drop or browse
- Skip option

**Step 5: Create First Classroom**
- Classroom name
- Teacher selection
- Grade level
- Generate access code

**Step 6: Confirmation**
- Summary of settings
- Portal URL
- First classroom created
- [View Portal] [Add More Classrooms]

---

### 3. Classroom Management (`portal/school-to-home/classrooms/`)

#### Classrooms List (`index.html`)

**Purpose**: View all classrooms across School-to-Home portals

**Table**:
| Classroom | Teacher | Grade | Students | Access Code | Portal | Actions |
|-----------|---------|-------|----------|-------------|--------|---------|

**Actions**:
- [+ Create Classroom] button
- Row actions: Edit, Delete, View Students

#### Create Classroom Modal (`create.html`)

**Form**:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           Create Classroom                  │
│                                             │
│ Classroom name *                            │
│ [Mrs. Johnson's 3rd Grade             ]    │
│                                             │
│ Teacher                                     │
│ [Select from roster...              ▼]      │
│ Or enter name: [                    ]       │
│                                             │
│ Grade level                                 │
│ [3rd Grade                          ▼]      │
│                                             │
│ Access code                                 │
│ [SPRING24] [🔄 Generate New]                │
│                                             │
│ [Cancel]                    [Create Classroom]
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Validation**:
- Name required
- Access code unique within portal
- Auto-generate or custom code

#### Classroom Detail (`detail.html`)

**Purpose**: View and manage a single classroom

**Content**:
- Classroom info (name, teacher, grade)
- Access code (with copy button)
- Student list (if tracked)
- Usage statistics
- Actions: Edit, Delete

---

### 4. Branding Configuration (`portal/school-to-home/branding.html`)

**Purpose**: Detailed branding settings for School-to-Home

**Sections**:
- Logo upload
- School name display
- Color customization (future)
- Preview of parent portal appearance

---

### 5. Billing (`portal/billing/`)

#### Stripe Portal Redirect (`redirect.html`)

**Purpose**: Redirect to Stripe Customer Portal for billing management

**Content**:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Manage Your Billing                        │
│                                             │
│  You'll be redirected to our secure billing│
│  portal where you can:                      │
│                                             │
│  • View and download invoices               │
│  • Update payment method                    │

### 6. Settings (`portal/settings/`)

#### Administrators (`administrators.html`)

**Purpose**: Manage who has admin access

## Deliverables

title: Build impersonation feature
description:
# Build Impersonation Feature

Implement 'View as Organization' for admin users to see the Portal from an organization's perspective.

## Implementation

### Admin Side (Trigger)

**On Organizations List:**
- Add 'Impersonate' action in row dropdown menu
- Confirm modal: 'View as {org name}?'

**On Organization Detail:**
- Add 'View as Organization' button in header
- Same confirm modal

### State Management

Use `state.js` impersonation object:
```javascript
impersonation: {
  active: false,
  orgId: null,
  orgName: null,
  startedAt: null
}
```

### Portal Side (Banner)

**Yellow banner at page top when impersonation active:**
- Text: 'Viewing as: {Organization Name}'
- 'Exit' button returns to Admin CRM
- Banner persists across all Portal pages

### Exit Flow

1. Click 'Exit' on banner
2. Clear impersonation state
3. Redirect to Admin org detail page

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Impersonate action visible in org list row menu
- [ ] Impersonate button on org detail page
- [ ] Confirm modal shows org name
- [ ] Clicking confirm sets state and redirects to Portal
- [ ] Yellow banner visible on all Portal pages
- [ ] Banner shows correct org name
- [ ] Exit button clears state
- [ ] Exit redirects to Admin org detail

## Verification

1. Go to Organizations list, click Impersonate on a row
2. Confirm modal appears, click 'View as Organization'
3. Redirected to Portal dashboard
4. Yellow banner visible at top
5. Navigate between Portal pages - banner persists
6. Click Exit - returned to Admin org detail

title: Phase 4: Organization Portal Core
description:
# Phase 4: Organization Portal Core ## Purpose Build the customer-facing Organization Portal that school administrators and teachers use to manage their licenses, seats, and users. This phase covers the essential daily-use pages: 1. **Portal Layout** — Calico-branded header, navigation, footer 2. **Dashboard** — Organization overview with onboarding tutorial 3. **Licenses** — View and manage purchased licenses 4. **Seats** — Assign, reassign, and revoke seat access --- ## Design Philosophy ### Customer-Facing Brand The Organization Portal uses Calico Spanish's customer-facing brand (different from Admin CRM): ```css /* Portal Colors */ --primary: #9fcc3f; /* Calico Green */ --secondary: #68c9c9; /* Calico Teal */ --accent: #482d8b; /* Calico Purple */ --background: #f8fafc; /* Light slate */ /* Gamification Level Colors */ --level-green: #9fcc3f; --level-teal: #68c9c9; --level-red: #f05759; --level-yellow: #fad358; ``` ### Simpler Layout Portal uses a **simpler 2-column layout** (not 3-column like Admin): - Primary navigation in left sidebar - Main content area - No right sidebar (less information density for customers) ### Friendly Language Error messages and instructions use approachable language: - "Oops! Something went wrong" not "Error 500" - "No seats assigned yet" with helpful next step - Tutorial prompts for new users --- ## Pages to Build ### 1. Portal Layout Template **Header**: ```html
Dashboard Licenses Seats Roster School-to-Home
``` **Sidebar**: - Dashboard - Licenses - Seats - Roster - School-to-Home (badge: "Premium" if enabled) - Billing - Settings - Help & Support **Footer**: - Copyright - Privacy Policy - Terms of Service - Contact Support ### 2. Dashboard (`portal/index.html`) **Purpose**: First screen customers see — overview + onboarding **Sections**: 1. **Onboarding Tutorial** (collapsible) - Progress tracker (3-5 steps) - Steps: 1. Review your licenses 2. Invite your teachers 3. Assign seats 4. Set up School-to-Home (Premium only) 5. Explore curriculum - Checkmarks for completed steps - "Dismiss tutorial" option (persists in state) 2. **License Summary Cards** - One card per license pool - Shows: Plan type, Seats (used/total), Expiration - Status indicator (healthy, expiring, at-risk) - Quick action: "Manage Seats" 3. **Seat Utilization Overview** - Visual bar showing overall utilization - Available seats count highlighted - "Assign Seats" CTA if seats available 4. **Quick Actions Grid** (2x2 or 2x3) - Invite a Teacher - Assign a Seat - View Licenses - Set up School-to-Home (if Premium) - Get Help 5. **Recent Activity Feed** - Last 5 organization activities - Format: "[User] [action] - [time ago]" - "View all activity" link 6. **Notifications/Alerts** - Expiring licenses warning - Pending invitations count - Low utilization notice ### 3. Licenses List (`portal/licenses/index.html`) **Purpose**: View all purchased licenses with filtering **Filter Bar**: - Type: All, Base, Premium - Duration: All, 1-Year, 3-Year, 5-Year, Custom - Status: All, Active, Expiring Soon, At-Risk, Expired - Sort: Expiration (soonest/latest), Seats (most/least), Type **License Cards** (not a table — card-based for friendliness): ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [PREMIUM] [3-YEAR] │ │ │ │ 50 seats │ │ ███████████░░░░░ 84% utilized │ │ 42 used · 8 available │ │ │ │ Expires: Dec 31, 2024 (245 days) │ │ [Active] ● │ │ │ │ [View Details] [Manage Seats] [Renew] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Card Elements**: - Type badge (Base = outline, Premium = filled green) - Duration badge - Seat count with utilization bar - Available seats highlighted - Expiration with countdown - Status indicator (color-coded) - Actions: View, Manage Seats, Renew, Upgrade (Base only) **Empty State**: "No licenses match your filters" with "Clear Filters" link ### 4. License Detail (`portal/licenses/detail.html`) **Purpose**: Deep dive into a single license **URL Pattern**: `detail.html?id=pool_001` **Header Section**: - Back link to licenses list - License type and duration badges - Status badge - Primary action: "Manage Seats" **Stats Cards Row**: - Total Seats - Seats Used - Seats Available - Days Remaining **Utilization Visualization**: - Large progress bar or donut chart - Percentage prominently displayed - Color-coded (green/yellow/red) **Assigned Users Table**: | Column | Description | |--------|-------------| | User | Name and email | | Role | Admin, Member | | Assigned Date | When seat was assigned | | Status | Active, Pending | | Actions | Reassign, Revoke | **Filter/Search**: Search by name or email **Pagination**: Show 10/25/50 per page **School-to-Home Section** (Premium only): - Status: Enabled/Not Configured - Link to portal configuration - Quick stats if enabled (classrooms, students) **Renewal/Upgrade Options**: - For Active licenses: "Request Renewal" (opens flow) - For Base licenses: "Upgrade to Premium" (explains benefits) ### 5. License Renew Flow (`portal/licenses/renew.html`) **Purpose**: Request license renewal **Steps**: 1. **Review Current License** - Current plan details - Expiration date - Seat count 2. **Select Renewal Option** - Same plan (recommended) - Different duration (1Y/3Y/5Y) - Add seats (optional) - Upgrade to Premium (if Base) 3. **Review Summary** - New term dates - Pricing estimate (if displayed) - Changes summary 4. **Submit Request** - Contact info confirmation - Notes/questions field - Submit button 5. **Confirmation** - "Request submitted" message - What happens next - Contact for questions *Note: This is a REQUEST flow, not direct purchase — Calico handles renewal offline* ### 6. License Upgrade Flow (`portal/licenses/upgrade.html`) **Purpose**: Request upgrade from Base to Premium **Content**: 1. **Benefits Comparison** - Base features (list) - Premium features (list with highlights) - Key difference: School-to-Home access 2. **Upgrade Form** - Current license info - Desired upgrade date - Contact info - Questions/notes 3. **Confirmation** - Request submitted - Calico will contact within X days ### 7. Seats Overview (`portal/seats/index.html`) **Purpose**: Centralized seat management across all licenses **Stats Row**: - Total Seats (all licenses combined) - Seats Used - Seats Available - Utilization % **License Breakdown Table**: | License | Plan | Total | Used | Available | Actions | |---------|------|-------|------|-----------|---------| | Pool 001 | Premium | 50 | 42 | 8 | Manage | | Pool 002 | Base | 25 | 20 | 5 | Manage | **All Assigned Seats Table**: | User | Email | License | Assigned Date | Status | Actions | |------|-------|---------|---------------|--------|---------| **Filters**: - License: All, specific license - Status: All, Active, Pending **Actions**: - [+ Assign Seat] button (prominent) - [Bulk Assign] button - Row actions: Reassign, Revoke ### 8. Assign Seat Flow (`portal/seats/assign.html`) **Purpose**: Multi-step wizard to assign a seat to a user **Step 1: Select License** (if multiple) - Radio button list of licenses with available seats - Show available seat count - Skip if only one license with seats **Step 2: Select or Invite User** ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Choose an existing user or invite someone │ │ │ │ [Search roster... ] │ │ │ │ ○ Jane Doe (jane@school.edu) [+] │ │ ○ John Smith (john@school.edu) [+] │ │ ○ Mary Johnson (mary@school.edu) [+] │ │ │ │ ─────────── OR ─────────── │ │ │ │ Invite new user: │ │ [email@example.com ] │ │ [First name ] [Last name ] │ │ │ │ [Back] [Continue] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Step 3: Confirm Assignment** - Summary of selection - License being assigned - User receiving seat - Confirmation checkbox (optional) - [Assign Seat] button **Step 4: Success State** ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ✓ Seat Assigned! │ │ │ │ Jane Doe now has access to Premium license │ │ │ │ What would you like to do next? │ │ │ │ [Assign Another Seat] [View All Seats] │ │ [Invite More Users] [Back to Dashboard] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Edge Cases**: - No seats available → Show message, link to request more - User already has seat → Show warning, offer reassign - Email already pending → Show status ### 9. Bulk Assign Flow (`portal/seats/bulk-assign.html`) **Purpose**: Assign multiple seats at once **Steps**: 1. Select license pool 2. Select multiple users (checkboxes) 3. Review selections 4. Confirm bulk assignment 5. Success with summary ### 10. Reassign Seat Flow (`portal/seats/reassign.html`) **Purpose**: Move a seat from one user to another **URL Pattern**: `reassign.html?seatId=xxx` **Content**: - Current assignment info - Reason for reassign (optional) - Select new user (same flow as assign) - Confirm reassignment - Success state --- ## Deliverables 1. `portal/index.html` — Dashboard with onboarding 2. `portal/licenses/index.html` — License cards with filters 3. `portal/licenses/detail.html` — License detail with seats table 4. `portal/licenses/renew.html` — Renewal request flow 5. `portal/licenses/upgrade.html` — Upgrade request flow 6. `portal/seats/index.html` — Seats overview 7. `portal/seats/assign.html` — Assign seat wizard 8. `portal/seats/bulk-assign.html` — Bulk assignment 9. `portal/seats/reassign.html` — Reassign seat 10. `shared/templates/partials/sidebar.portal.html` --- ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Dashboard loads with onboarding tutorial visible - [ ] Onboarding progress persists after dismissal - [ ] License cards show correct utilization bars - [ ] License filters update cards in real-time - [ ] License detail shows all assigned users - [ ] Assign seat flow completes all 4 steps - [ ] Success state shows appropriate next actions - [ ] Edge cases (no seats, existing assignment) handled gracefully - [ ] All links navigate correctly - [ ] Mobile responsive at 375px width
acceptance_criteria:
**Code is Functional WHEN:**
- Portal layout renders with Calico branding
- Portal dashboard shows onboarding and license cards
- Licenses list filters by type/status
- Seat management pages display correctly
- All portal navigation links work

description:
# Phase 3: Admin CRM Extended

## Purpose

Complete the Admin CRM with secondary but important functionality:

1. **Billing Accounts** — Financial relationship management
2. **Analytics Dashboard** — Data visualization for business insights
3. **Orders** — Transaction history
4. **Audit Logs** — System activity tracking for compliance
5. **Settings** — System configuration

These pages are less frequently used than Phase 2 pages but essential for a complete admin experience.

---

## Pages to Build

### 1. Billing Accounts (`admin/billing-accounts/`)

#### Billing Accounts List (`index.html`)

**Purpose**: View all billing relationships across organizations

**Table Columns**:
| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Account ID | Billing account identifier |
| Organization | Linked organization name |
| Stripe Customer | Stripe customer ID |
| Payment Method | Card type + last 4 digits |
| Status | Active, Suspended, None |
| Outstanding Balance | Current amount due |
| Last Invoice | Date of most recent invoice |
| Actions | View, Edit, View in Stripe |

**Filters**:
- Status: All, Active, Suspended, No Payment Method
- Outstanding Balance: All, Has Balance, No Balance
- Payment Method: All, Card, Bank, None

#### Billing Account Detail (`detail.html`)

**Left Sidebar**:
- Account ID
- Organization link
- Stripe Customer ID (link to Stripe dashboard)
- Status badge
- Payment method info
  - Type (Visa, Mastercard, etc.)
  - Last 4 digits
  - Expiration
- Outstanding balance

**Center Column (Tabs)**:
1. **Overview Tab**
   - Account summary cards
   - Recent payment activity
   - Quick actions (send invoice, update payment method)

2. **Invoices Tab**
   - Full invoice history table
   - Columns: Invoice #, Date, Amount, Status, Due Date, Actions
   - Actions: View, Download PDF, Resend, Mark Paid (manual)
   - Filter by status (Paid, Open, Past Due, Void)

3. **Payments Tab**
   - Payment transaction history
   - Columns: Date, Amount, Method, Status, Invoice
   - Filter by status (Succeeded, Failed, Refunded)

4. **Activity Tab**
   - Billing-specific activity log
   - Payment method changes, invoice events, etc.

**Right Sidebar**:
- Organization card
- License Pools summary
- Upcoming renewals

#### Invoices View (`invoices.html`)

Cross-organization invoice search and management:
- Table of all invoices across all accounts
- Filters: Status, Date Range, Amount Range
- Bulk actions: Download, Resend, Export

---

### 2. Analytics Dashboard (`admin/analytics/`)

#### Main Analytics (`index.html`)

**Purpose**: High-level business metrics with interactive charts

**KPI Cards Row** (4 cards):
- Total MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue)
- Total ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue)
- Active License Count
- At-Risk Pool Count

**Charts Section**:

1. **Revenue Over Time** (Line Chart)
   - Toggle: MRR / ARR
   - Range: 6M, 1Y, All Time
   - Tooltip shows exact values
   - Chart.js implementation

2. **License Utilization** (Gauge/Donut)
   - Overall utilization percentage
   - Color zones: Green (80%+), Yellow (50-80%), Red (<50%)

3. **New vs Renewed Licenses** (Stacked Bar)
   - Monthly comparison
   - Shows growth vs retention

4. **Seats by Plan Type** (Donut/Pie)
   - Base vs Premium breakdown
   - Show counts and percentages

5. **At-Risk Pools Trend** (Line)
   - Track at-risk pool count over time
   - Goal: trending downward

**Chart.js Implementation Notes**:
```javascript
// Use Chart.js 4.x canvas-based charts
// Responsive: true for all charts
// Maintain aspect ratio: false (control height via container)
// Tooltips: Show formatted values
// Legend: Position varies by chart type
```

#### Revenue Analytics (`revenue.html`)

Deep dive into revenue metrics:
- Revenue by organization type (School/District/Co-op)
- Revenue by plan type (Base/Premium)
- Revenue by duration (1Y/3Y/5Y)
- Cohort analysis (revenue by signup month)

#### License Analytics (`licenses.html`)

License-specific metrics:
- Utilization histogram
- License type distribution
- Expiration timeline
- Churn analysis

#### Customer Analytics (`customers.html`)

Customer-centric metrics:
- Organization growth over time
- Customer lifetime value
- Churn rate
- Net promoter (if available)

---

### 3. Orders (`admin/orders/`)

#### Orders List (`index.html`)

**Purpose**: Track all purchase transactions

**Table Columns**:
| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Order ID | Unique order identifier |
| Date | Order timestamp |
| Organization | Customer |
| Items | License pools purchased |
| Total | Order amount |
| Status | Completed, Pending, Failed, Refunded |
| Payment | Stripe charge ID |
| Actions | View, Refund |

**Filters**:
- Status: All, Completed, Pending, Failed, Refunded
- Date Range: Preset and custom
- Amount Range: Min/Max

#### Order Detail (`detail.html`)

**Content**:
- Order header (ID, date, status)
- Customer info (org name, contact)
- Line items table (licenses, seats, prices)
- Payment info (method, transaction ID)
- Timeline (order events)
- Actions (refund, resend receipt)

---

### 4. Audit Logs (`admin/audit-logs/`)

#### Audit Log Viewer (`index.html`)

**Purpose**: Compliance and debugging — who did what, when

**Table Columns**:
| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Timestamp | ISO 8601 format |
| Actor | User who performed action |
| Action | What happened |
| Entity Type | Organization, License, User, etc. |
| Entity ID | Link to entity |
| IP Address | Request origin |
| Details | Expandable JSON diff |

**Filters**:
- Actor: User selector
- Action: CRUD operations
- Entity Type: Organization, LicensePool, User, etc.
- Date Range: Preset and custom

**Features**:
- Infinite scroll pagination
- Detail expansion (click row to see full JSON)
- Export to CSV/JSON
- Real-time updates (visual indicator only in mockup)

---

### 5. Settings (`admin/settings/`)

#### Settings Overview (`index.html`)

Card-based navigation to setting sections:
- Plans & Pricing
- Integrations
- Email Templates
- Notifications
- Team & Permissions

#### Plans Configuration (`plans.html`)

**Purpose**: Configure license plan options

**Plan Cards**:
- Base Plan
  - Features list
  - Default pricing
  - Duration options
- Premium Plan
  - Features list (includes School-to-Home)
  - Default pricing
  - Duration options

**Editable Fields**:
- Plan name
- Description
- Feature list (add/remove)
- Pricing tiers
- Availability (Active/Inactive)

*Note: Changes are mockup only — no actual backend*

#### Integrations (`integrations.html`)

**Integration Cards**:

1. **Stripe**
   - Status: Connected/Not Connected
   - Webhook status
   - API key (masked)
   - Actions: Configure, Test Connection

2. **Google Workspace** (future)
   - Status: Not Configured
   - SSO settings placeholder

3. **Clever** (future)
   - Status: Not Configured
   - Roster sync placeholder

---

## Deliverables

1. `admin/billing-accounts/index.html`
2. `admin/billing-accounts/detail.html`
3. `admin/billing-accounts/invoices.html`
4. `admin/analytics/index.html`
5. `admin/analytics/revenue.html`
6. `admin/analytics/licenses.html`
7. `admin/analytics/customers.html`
8. `admin/orders/index.html`
9. `admin/orders/detail.html`
10. `admin/audit-logs/index.html`
11. `admin/settings/index.html`
12. `admin/settings/plans.html`
13. `admin/settings/integrations.html`

---

## Chart.js Integration Notes

```javascript
// shared/js/charts.js

// Register Chart.js components
import { Chart, registerables } from "chart.js";
Chart.register(...registerables);

// Create reusable chart configurations
export function createLineChart(ctx, data, options) {
  return new Chart(ctx, {
    type: "line",
    data,
    options: {
      responsive: true,
      maintainAspectRatio: false,
      plugins: {
        legend: { position: "top" },
        tooltip: { mode: "index", intersect: false }
      },
      ...options
    }
  });
}

// Similar factories for bar, donut, gauge charts
```

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Billing accounts list loads with payment status indicators
- [ ] Billing account detail shows invoice history in tab
- [ ] Analytics dashboard renders all 5 charts (no errors)
- [ ] Charts respond to container size (responsive)
- [ ] Orders list pagination works
- [ ] Audit logs infinite scroll loads more rows
- [ ] Settings pages display current configuration
- [ ] All navigation links work

- Billing accounts list/detail pages render correctly
- Analytics dashboard shows charts (Chart.js working)
- Orders and audit logs pages display data
- Admin settings pages are navigable
- All links between extended pages work

title: Calico Spanish CRM & Portal HTML Mockups
description:
# Calico Spanish CRM & Organization Portal - Complete HTML Mockups

## Executive Summary

This epic encompasses the complete development of high-fidelity, Figma-level interactive HTML mockups for the Calico Spanish Membership & Licensing CRM system. These mockups serve as the definitive design approval artifacts BEFORE any production implementation begins.

---

## Business Context & Rationale

### What is Calico Spanish?

Calico Spanish is a language learning company that sells curriculum licenses to educational institutions. Their business model involves:

- **B2B Sales**: Selling annual/multi-year license subscriptions to schools, districts, and homeschool co-ops
- **Seat-Based Licensing**: Each organization buys a "pool" of seats that can be assigned to individual teachers/students
- **Premium Tiers**: Base licenses include core curriculum; Premium adds "School-to-Home" parent portal access
- **Recurring Revenue**: Subscription renewals are a key metric (MRR/ARR tracking)

### Why Two Portals?

1. **Internal Admin CRM** — Used by Calico Spanish employees to:
   - Manage customer organizations (schools, districts, co-ops)
   - Track billing accounts and invoices
   - Monitor license pool health (utilization, expiration, at-risk)
   - View analytics and reports
   - Provide customer support

2. **Organization Portal** — Used by customers (school administrators, teachers) to:
   - View and manage their purchased licenses
   - Assign seats to teachers/students
   - Manage their user roster (invite, remove users)
   - Configure School-to-Home parent portals (Premium only)
   - Update organization settings

### Why Mockups First? (Strategic Decision)

Building static HTML mockups BEFORE the production React application provides several critical benefits:

1. **Stakeholder Alignment (Primary)**: Visual mockups enable non-technical stakeholders (CEO, product owners, sales) to review and approve UX before expensive development begins
2. **Reduced Rework (Cost Savings)**: Catching design issues in HTML costs ~10% of fixing them in React; this prevents the "build-then-redo" cycle
3. **Design System Foundation**: Establishes patterns, tokens, components, and interactions that will be directly translated to React
4. **Fast Iteration**: Static HTML with Tailwind iterates 5-10x faster than React components during the design exploration phase
5. **Documentation**: The mockups themselves serve as living documentation of the intended UX

---

## Tech Stack Decisions

### Mockup Stack (This Epic)

| Package | Version | Purpose | Why This Choice |
|---------|---------|---------|-----------------|
| Vite | 5.x | Dev server + build | ESM-native, instant HMR, zero config |
| Tailwind CSS | 4.x | Styling | CSS-first config, @theme tokens |
| Chart.js | 4.x | Analytics charts | Lightweight, canvas-based |
| Flatpickr | 4.x | Date pickers | Most accessible, no deps |
| Lucide Icons | Latest | Icon system | Same as shadcn/ui (future consistency) |

**Font**: Inter (Google Fonts) with `font-display: swap`

**Browser Support**: Chrome 100+, Firefox 100+, Safari 15+, Edge 100+

### Future Production Stack (Phase 7, NOT this epic)

After mockup approval, the production app will use:
- Next.js 16 with TypeScript
- React 19
- shadcn/ui components
- Laravel backend APIs

The mockups establish the visual language that React will implement.

---

## Brand & Design System

### Admin CRM Colors (Neutral/Professional)

```css
--primary: #482d8b;      /* Calico Purple (from brand) */
--sidebar: #1e1b4b;      /* Dark Indigo */
--accent: #9fcc3f;       /* Calico Green */
--background: #f8fafc;   /* Slate 50 */
```

### Organization Portal Colors (Customer-Facing)

```css
--primary: #9fcc3f;      /* Calico Green */
--secondary: #68c9c9;    /* Calico Teal */
--accent: #482d8b;       /* Calico Purple */
/* Level Colors for gamification: */
--level-green: #9fcc3f;
--level-teal: #68c9c9;
--level-red: #f05759;
--level-yellow: #fad358;
```

### Layout Philosophy

Both portals follow a **HubSpot-inspired 3-column layout** for detail views:

| Column | Width | Content |
|--------|-------|---------|
| Left Sidebar | ~25% | About section, key metrics, tags |
| Center | ~50% | Tabs with primary content |
| Right Sidebar | ~25% | Related entities, associations |

This layout provides context without navigation, reducing cognitive load.

---

## File Structure

```
laravel-crm-mockups/
├── src/
│   ├── shared/
│   │   ├── styles/          # Tailwind config, base CSS, tokens
│   │   ├── js/              # Components (modal, dropdown, toast)
│   │   ├── data/            # Seed JSON fixtures
│   │   └── templates/       # Partials (header, sidebar)
│   ├── admin/               # ~25 HTML pages
│   │   ├── index.html       # Dashboard
│   │   ├── organizations/   # CRUD + detail
│   │   ├── billing-accounts/
│   │   ├── license-pools/
│   │   ├── users/
│   │   ├── orders/
│   │   ├── analytics/
│   │   ├── audit-logs/
│   │   └── settings/
│   └── portal/              # ~20 HTML pages
│       ├── index.html       # Dashboard
│       ├── licenses/
│       ├── seats/
│       ├── roster/
│       ├── school-to-home/
│       ├── billing/
│       └── settings/
└── dist/                    # Build output
```

---

## Success Criteria (Definition of Done)

1. **Complete Navigation**: All 45-50 HTML pages linked and navigable
2. **Interactive Elements**: All modals, dropdowns, forms, tabs functional
3. **Data Persistence**: Demo data persists via LocalStorage across pages
4. **Responsive**: Works at 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop)
5. **Accessible**: Zero critical violations in axe-core audit
6. **Verified**: Playwright smoke tests pass
7. **Approved**: Stakeholder sign-off on all critical paths

---

## Critical User Journeys (Must Work)

1. **Admin: Org Management**
   Dashboard → Organizations List → Organization Detail → Edit/Impersonate

2. **Admin: License Monitoring**
   Dashboard → At-Risk Pools → Pool Detail → Contact Org

3. **Portal: Seat Assignment**
   Dashboard → Licenses → Assign Seat → Select User → Confirm

4. **Portal: School-to-Home Setup**
   Dashboard → School-to-Home → Setup Wizard → Configure → Create Classroom

---

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Name | Tasks | Dependencies |
|-------|------|-------|--------------|
| 0 | Build Pipeline Setup | 5 | None |
| 1 | Shared Infrastructure | 5+ | Phase 0 |
| 2 | Admin CRM Core | 7+ | Phase 1 |
| 3 | Admin CRM Extended | 4+ | Phase 2 |
| 4 | Portal Core | 4+ | Phase 1 |
| 5 | Portal Extended | 4+ | Phase 4 |
| 6 | Polish & Verification | 8 | Phase 3, 5 |

**Parallel Opportunity**: Phases 2-3 (Admin) and Phases 4-5 (Portal) can run in parallel after Phase 1.

---

## Reference Documents

- `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` — Canonical source (this bead derived from it)
- `AGENTS.md` — Agent rules and constraints
- `laravel-crm-mockups/README.md` — Dev setup instructions

title: Phase 0: Build Pipeline Setup
description:
# Phase 0: Build Pipeline Setup

## Purpose

Establish the foundational build tooling that all subsequent phases depend on. This phase creates the development environment, configures the build system, and verifies everything works before any UI development begins.

## Why This Phase First?

Without a working build pipeline:
- CSS will not compile (Tailwind needs PostCSS)
- No hot module reload (slower development iteration)
- No production build for stakeholder preview
- Dependencies cannot be imported

This is the "foundation" — everything else builds on top of it.

---

## Design Decisions Made

### Why Vite (Not Webpack/Parcel)?

- **Zero Config**: Works out of the box for HTML/CSS/JS projects
- **Speed**: ESM-native dev server, instant HMR
- **Simplicity**: No complex webpack.config.js to maintain
- **Future-Compatible**: Same tool used in Next.js/React ecosystem

### Why Tailwind CSS 4.x?

Tailwind 4 introduces a **CSS-first configuration** approach:
- Design tokens defined in CSS with `@theme { }` directive
- No `tailwind.config.js` needed for basic customization
- Easier migration to any future framework
- Tokens live in the same file as styles (single source of truth)

**Note**: If Tailwind 4 has compatibility issues, falling back to v3 with JS config is acceptable — the mockup fidelity matters more than the config format.

### Why These Specific Versions?

| Package | Version | Rationale |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| vite@^5.0.0 | Stable major | Avoid bleeding edge |
| tailwindcss@^4.0.0 | Latest paradigm | CSS-first benefits |
| chart.js@^4.0.0 | Stable major | Analytics in Phase 3 |
| flatpickr@^4.6.0 | Most accessible | Date pickers throughout |
| lucide@latest | Icon consistency | Same as shadcn/ui |

---

## Deliverables

By the end of Phase 0:

1. **Directory Structure Created**
   ```
   laravel-crm-mockups/
   ├── package.json
   ├── vite.config.js
   ├── postcss.config.js
   ├── tailwind.config.js (or styles with @theme)
   └── src/
       └── index.html (test page)
   ```

2. **Commands Working**
   - `npm run dev` — Starts dev server with HMR
   - `npm run build` — Produces `/dist` folder
   - `npm run preview` — Serves production build locally

3. **Styling Working**
   - Tailwind classes compile correctly
   - Brand colors (purple #482d8b, green #9fcc3f) render
   - Custom design tokens accessible

4. **Dependencies Available**
   - Chart.js importable (verified with console.log)
   - Flatpickr CSS loadable
   - Lucide icons renderable

---

## Verification Checklist

```bash
# After completing Phase 0, run:
cd laravel-crm-mockups
npm run dev          # Should start on localhost:5173
# Open browser, verify:
# - Page loads without console errors
# - Tailwind classes work (e.g., bg-purple-600 shows color)
# - HMR works (edit file, see instant update)

npm run build        # Should complete without errors
ls dist/             # Should contain HTML/CSS/JS
npm run preview      # Should serve production build
```

---

## Risk Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Tailwind 4 breaking changes | Fall back to v3 with JS config |
| Vite version conflicts | Pin exact versions in package-lock.json |
| Chart.js import issues | Use CDN fallback if bundling fails |

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run dev` starts without errors
- [ ] `npm run build` completes without errors
- [ ] Browser shows styled page with Tailwind classes
- [ ] Brand colors render correctly (verify purple/green)
- [ ] All dependencies importable without errors
acceptance_criteria:
**Code is Functional WHEN:**
- `npm run dev` starts Vite dev server without errors
- `npm run build` completes and creates dist/ folder
- Tailwind classes compile (test with bg-purple-600)
- Hot module reload works (edit file → instant browser update)
- Chart.js, Flatpickr, Lucide are importable without errors
- Brand colors render correctly (#482d8b purple, #9fcc3f green)

title: Initialize package.json with Vite and Tailwind CSS 4.x
description:
# Initialize package.json with Vite and Tailwind CSS

## Goal

Create the foundation of the mockup project by initializing npm and installing all core dependencies.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create Directory Structure

```bash
mkdir -p laravel-crm-mockups/src/{shared/{styles,js,data,templates},admin,portal}
cd laravel-crm-mockups
```

### 2. Initialize npm

```bash
npm init -y
```

### 3. Install Core Dependencies

```bash
# Build tools (dev dependencies)
npm install -D vite@^5.0.0 tailwindcss@^4.0.0 postcss autoprefixer

# UI dependencies (runtime)
npm install chart.js@^4.0.0 flatpickr@^4.6.0 lucide@latest
```

### 4. Update package.json Scripts

```json
{
  "name": "laravel-crm-mockups",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.0",
    "postcss": "^8.4.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0",
    "vite": "^5.0.0"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "chart.js": "^4.0.0",
    "flatpickr": "^4.6.0",
    "lucide": "latest"
  }
}
```

---

## Package Selection Rationale

| Package | Version | Why This Version |
|---------|---------|------------------|
| vite | ^5.0.0 | Stable, ESM-native, instant HMR |
| tailwindcss | ^4.0.0 | CSS-first config, @theme tokens |
| postcss | ^8.4.0 | Required by Tailwind |
| autoprefixer | ^10.4.0 | Browser compatibility |
| chart.js | ^4.0.0 | Lightweight canvas charts |
| flatpickr | ^4.6.0 | Accessible date picker |
| lucide | latest | Same icons as shadcn/ui |

### Why Vite over Webpack/Parcel?

1. **Zero Config**: Works out of the box for HTML/CSS/JS
2. **Speed**: Native ESM in dev, esbuild for deps
3. **HMR**: Instant hot module replacement
4. **Ecosystem**: Same tool used in Next.js/React

### Why Tailwind 4.x?

Tailwind 4 introduces CSS-first configuration:
- Design tokens defined in CSS with `@theme { }`
- No JavaScript config file needed
- Easier to port to any future framework
- Single source of truth for design tokens

**Fallback**: If Tailwind 4 has issues, v3 with JS config is acceptable.

---

## Verification

After running npm install:

```bash
# Check node_modules exists
ls node_modules/vite node_modules/tailwindcss

# Verify package-lock.json created
ls package-lock.json
```

---

## Files Created

- `laravel-crm-mockups/package.json`
- `laravel-crm-mockups/package-lock.json`
- `laravel-crm-mockups/node_modules/` (git-ignored)

---

## Common Issues

1. **npm 7+ peer deps**: May need `--legacy-peer-deps` flag
2. **Tailwind 4 beta**: Check release notes if install fails
3. **lucide namespace**: Import as `import { icons } from "lucide"`

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] package.json exists with correct name and scripts
- [ ] All 6 dependencies listed in package.json
- [ ] `npm install` completes without errors
- [ ] node_modules/ directory populated

title: Configure vite.config.js for multi-page HTML
Set up Tailwind CSS with design tokens
description:
# Set Up Tailwind CSS

## Goal

Add Tailwind CSS for styling the HTML mockups. Keep it simple - this is for wireframes, not production.

## Implementation

### Option 1: CDN (Simplest)

```html
<!-- In each HTML file <head> -->
<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>
<script>
  tailwind.config = {
    theme: {
      extend: {
        colors: {
          'admin-primary': '#482d8b',
          'admin-sidebar': '#1e1b4b',
          'admin-accent': '#9fcc3f',
          'portal-primary': '#9fcc3f',
          'portal-secondary': '#68c9c9',
        }
      }
    }
  }
</script>
```

### Option 2: npm Install (If CDN causes issues)

```bash
npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer
npx tailwindcss init
```

## Font: Inter

```html
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
```

## Brand Colors

| Name | Hex | Usage |
|------|-----|-------|
| admin-primary | #482d8b | Calico Purple - admin buttons |
| admin-sidebar | #1e1b4b | Dark indigo sidebar |
| admin-accent | #9fcc3f | Calico Green highlights |
| portal-primary | #9fcc3f | Portal main color |
| portal-secondary | #68c9c9 | Calico Teal |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tailwind classes work (e.g., `bg-purple-600` renders)
- [ ] Custom colors work (e.g., `bg-admin-primary`)
- [ ] Inter font loads and displays
- [ ] No console errors

## Verification

Create test.html with colored divs, verify in browser.

# Verify Build Pipeline Works ## Goal Ensure the complete build pipeline functions correctly before proceeding to Phase 1. ## Verification Checklist ### 1. Development Server (`npm run dev`) ```bash cd laravel-crm-mockups npm run dev ``` **Expected Output**: ``` VITE v5.x.x ready in XXX ms ➜ Local: http://localhost:5173/admin/index.html ➜ Network: http://192.168.x.x:5173/admin/index.html ``` **Verify**: - [ ] Server starts without errors - [ ] Opens correct URL in browser - [ ] Page displays (even if blank/minimal) - [ ] No console errors in browser ### 2. Hot Module Replacement (HMR) **Test Process**: 1. With dev server running, open browser 2. Edit a CSS class in tokens.css 3. Change should apply instantly (no refresh) 4. Edit HTML file 5. Page should update immediately **Verify**: - [ ] CSS changes apply without refresh - [ ] HTML changes apply without refresh - [ ] No full page reloads for small changes ### 3. Production Build (`npm run build`) ```bash npm run build ``` **Expected Output**: ``` vite v5.x.x building for production... ✓ XX modules transformed. dist/admin/index.html X.XX kB dist/shared/styles/tokens.css X.XX kB ... ✓ built in X.XXs ``` **Verify**: - [ ] Build completes without errors - [ ] `dist/` folder created - [ ] All HTML files present in dist/ - [ ] CSS files compiled and minified - [ ] JS files bundled ### 4. Preview Production Build (`npm run preview`) ```bash npm run preview ``` **Test Process**: 1. Open preview URL in browser 2. Navigate through pages 3. Check that all assets load **Verify**: - [ ] Server starts on different port (4173) - [ ] Pages load correctly - [ ] Styles applied - [ ] No 404 errors for assets ### 5. Tailwind Processing **Create Test File**: ```html Admin Dashboard

Admin CRM

Dashboard

Build pipeline test page

Icons working
``` **Verify**: - [ ] `bg-admin-bg` applies light background - [ ] `bg-admin-sidebar` applies dark indigo - [ ] `bg-admin-primary` shows purple (#482d8b) - [ ] `bg-admin-accent` shows green (#9fcc3f) - [ ] Inter font applied to text - [ ] Custom border-radius classes work ### 6. Library Imports **Test in app.js**: ```javascript // src/shared/js/app.js import { createIcons, icons } from "lucide"; import Chart from "chart.js/auto"; import flatpickr from "flatpickr"; console.log("✓ Lucide icons loaded:", Object.keys(icons).length); console.log("✓ Chart.js loaded:", Chart.version); console.log("✓ Flatpickr loaded:", flatpickr.VERSION); // Initialize icons document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => { createIcons({ icons }); }); ``` **Verify in Browser Console**: - [ ] "✓ Lucide icons loaded: XXX" - [ ] "✓ Chart.js loaded: X.X.X" - [ ] "✓ Flatpickr loaded: X.X.X" - [ ] No import errors --- ## Troubleshooting ### Common Issues | Issue | Solution | |-------|----------| | "Cannot find module" | Check import paths, verify package installed | | Styles not applying | Verify CSS import in HTML, check Tailwind config | | Build fails with ENOENT | Check all file paths in vite.config.js | | HMR not working | Check websocket connection, restart dev server | | Icons not showing | Verify lucide import, check createIcons called | ### Debug Commands ```bash # Check installed packages npm list vite tailwindcss chart.js flatpickr lucide # Clear cache and reinstall rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json npm install # Verbose build npm run build -- --debug ``` --- ## Smoke Test Script ```bash #!/bin/bash # scripts/verify-build.sh echo "🔍 Verifying build pipeline..." # Check npm packages echo "Checking dependencies..." npm list vite tailwindcss chart.js flatpickr lucide > /dev/null || exit 1 echo "✓ Dependencies OK" # Run dev server briefly echo "Testing dev server..." timeout 10 npm run dev & sleep 5 curl -s http://localhost:5173/admin/index.html > /dev/null || exit 1 pkill -f "vite" echo "✓ Dev server OK" # Run build echo "Testing production build..." npm run build > /dev/null || exit 1 [ -d "dist" ] || exit 1 echo "✓ Build OK" # Check output files echo "Verifying build output..." [ -f "dist/admin/index.html" ] || exit 1 echo "✓ Output files OK" echo "🎉 Build pipeline verified successfully!" ``` --- ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `npm run dev` starts without errors - [ ] HMR updates CSS and HTML instantly - [ ] `npm run build` completes without errors - [ ] `npm run preview` serves production build - [ ] All Tailwind brand colors render correctly - [ ] All three libraries import without errors - [ ] No console errors in browser

title: Write Phase 0 verification tests
description:
# Phase 0 Verification Tests

## Purpose

Establish testable verification criteria BEFORE building. TDD approach ensures we know exactly when Phase 0 is "done."

## Code is Functional WHEN...

### Build Pipeline Tests

```bash
# Test 1: npm install succeeds
npm install 2>&1 | grep -q "added" && echo "PASS: Dependencies installed"

# Test 2: Dev server starts
timeout 10 npm run dev &
sleep 3
curl -s http://localhost:5173 | grep -q "<!DOCTYPE" && echo "PASS: Dev server responds"
pkill -f vite

# Test 3: Production build succeeds
npm run build && [ -d "dist" ] && echo "PASS: Build output exists"

# Test 4: Tailwind processes custom colors
grep -q "482d8b" dist/**/*.css && echo "PASS: Brand colors in CSS"

# Test 5: Libraries importable
node -e "import('chart.js').then(() => console.log('PASS: Chart.js'))"
```

### Manual Verification Checklist

- [ ] `npm run dev` starts in < 5 seconds
- [ ] Browser shows page without console errors
- [ ] HMR updates CSS without refresh
- [ ] `npm run build` completes in < 30 seconds
- [ ] `dist/` contains HTML, CSS, JS files
- [ ] Purple (#482d8b) and Green (#9fcc3f) render correctly

## Edge Cases / Pointy Bits

1. **Tailwind v4 vs v3**: If v4 CSS-first config has issues, document and fallback to v3
2. **Node version**: Requires Node 18+; document in README
3. **Port conflicts**: Dev server should handle port 5173 busy gracefully
4. **Windows paths**: Vite config paths may need adjustment for Windows

## Acceptance Criteria

This bead is DONE when:
- [ ] All 5 automated tests pass
- [ ] All manual checks verified
- [ ] Edge cases documented in README
- [ ] Any workarounds noted in bead comments

title: Build Modal component with accessibility
description:
# Build Modal Component ## Purpose Reusable modal dialog component with full accessibility support. ## File Location `src/shared/js/components/modal.js` ## API ```javascript Modal.open(id) // Opens modal by data-modal-id Modal.close(id) // Closes specific modal Modal.closeAll() // Closes all open modals ``` ## Required Features 1. **Trigger Handling** - Button with `data-modal-target="modal-id"` opens modal - Multiple triggers can target same modal 2. **Close Behaviors** - Close button (X) inside modal - Backdrop click - Escape key - Programmatic Modal.close() 3. **Accessibility** - `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"` - Focus trapped inside modal while open - Focus returns to trigger on close - Body scroll locked when open - Announce to screen readers 4. **Animation** - Fade in/out backdrop - Scale in/out modal content - 200ms transition duration ## HTML Structure ```html

Create Organization

``` ## CSS Classes ```css .modal { /* Full screen fixed container */ } .modal.hidden { display: none; } .modal-backdrop { /* Semi-transparent overlay */ } .modal-content { /* Centered white box */ } .modal-header { /* Title + close button */ } .modal-body { /* Scrollable content area */ } .modal-footer { /* Action buttons */ } ``` ## Focus Trap Implementation ```javascript function trapFocus(element) { const focusable = element.querySelectorAll( "button, [href], input, select, textarea, [tabindex]:not([tabindex=\"-1\"])" ); const first = focusable[0]; const last = focusable[focusable.length - 1]; element.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => { if (e.key === "Tab") { if (e.shiftKey && document.activeElement === first) { e.preventDefault(); last.focus(); } else if (!e.shiftKey && document.activeElement === last) { e.preventDefault(); first.focus(); } } }); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Modal opens on trigger click - [ ] Modal closes with X, backdrop, Escape - [ ] Focus trapped inside modal - [ ] Focus returns to trigger on close - [ ] Body scroll locked when open - [ ] Works with multiple modals on page

title: Phase 2: Admin CRM Core
description:
# Phase 2: Admin CRM Core ## Purpose Build the essential Admin CRM pages that Calico Spanish employees will use daily to manage customer organizations, license pools, and users. This phase focuses on the most critical workflows: 1. **Dashboard** — Overview of system health and attention items 2. **Organizations** — CRUD and detail views for customer accounts 3. **License Pools** — Monitoring seat utilization and expiration 4. **Users** — Managing contacts across organizations 5. **Impersonation** — Viewing the portal as a customer would see it --- ## Design Philosophy ### HubSpot-Style 3-Column Layout Detail pages follow a proven pattern from HubSpot CRM: ``` ┌─────────────┬──────────────────────────┬─────────────┐ │ │ │ │ │ LEFT │ CENTER │ RIGHT │ │ SIDEBAR │ (TABS) │ SIDEBAR │ │ │ │ │ │ - About │ [Overview] [Billing] │ - Related │ │ - Metrics │ [Users] [Activity] │ Entities │ │ - Tags │ │ - Actions │ │ - Notes │ Tab content area... │ - Links │ │ │ │ │ └─────────────┴──────────────────────────┴─────────────┘ ``` **Why 3-Column?** - Context always visible (left sidebar) - Primary content gets most space (center) - Related entities accessible (right sidebar) - Reduces need to navigate away ### Full-Featured Tables All list pages use data tables with: - **Sorting**: Click column header to sort (asc/desc toggle) - **Filtering**: Dropdown filters above table - **Search**: Quick search within results - **Bulk Selection**: Checkbox column + bulk actions - **Pagination**: Page size selector (10/25/50/100) - **Column Visibility**: Toggle columns on/off - **Saved Views**: Save filter combinations (mockup only) --- ## Pages to Build ### 1. Dashboard (`admin/index.html`) **Layout**: Single-column with card grid **Sections**: 1. **Welcome Banner** - Greeting with user name - Quick action buttons (New Organization, View At-Risk) 2. **Attention Items** (Red/Yellow alerts) - At-risk license pools (< 30 days, low utilization) - Failed payment attempts - Expiring licenses this month - Pending support requests 3. **Quick Stats** (4-column card row) - Active Organizations (count) - Total Seats Licensed (count) - Utilization Rate (percentage) - MRR (dollar amount) 4. **Recent Activity Feed** - Last 10 actions across all entities - Format: "[User] [action] [entity] - [time ago]" **No Charts**: Dashboard focuses on actionable items; charts live in Analytics (Phase 3) ### 2. Organizations List (`admin/organizations/index.html`) **Table Columns**: | Column | Sortable | Filterable | |--------|----------|------------| | Checkbox | - | - | | Name | Yes | Yes (search) | | Type | Yes | Yes (School/District/Co-op) | | Status | Yes | Yes (Active/Inactive/Suspended) | | Active Licenses | Yes | - | | Total Seats | Yes | Yes (range) | | Utilization % | Yes | Yes (range) | | Primary Contact | - | - | | Created | Yes | Yes (date range) | | Actions | - | - | **Filters**: - Type: School, District, Co-op, All - Status: Active, Inactive, Suspended, All - Utilization: All, Under 50%, 50-80%, Over 80% - Has Premium: Yes, No, All - Created: Date range picker **Row Actions Dropdown**: - View Details - Quick Edit (modal) - Impersonate (view as org) - Add License - Archive **Bulk Actions** (when rows selected): - Export Selected (CSV) - Bulk Tag - Bulk Archive ### 3. Organization Detail (`admin/organizations/detail.html`) **URL Pattern**: `detail.html?id=org_001` **Left Sidebar (About)**: - Organization name (editable inline) - Type badge (School/District/Co-op) - Status badge with color - Primary contact (name, email, phone) - Key metrics: - Total Seats: 150 - Used Seats: 120 - Utilization: 80% - MRR: $450.00 - Tags (clickable, editable) - Internal notes (collapsible text area) **Center Column (Tabs)**: 1. **Overview Tab** - Summary cards (licenses, seats, billing status) - Recent activity (last 5 items) - Quick actions grid 2. **License Pools Tab** - Table of all pools for this org - Columns: Pool ID, Plan, Seats, Utilization, Expires, Status - Actions: View, Extend, Add Seats 3. **Billing Tab** - Billing account info - Payment method on file - Recent invoices table - Link to billing account detail 4. **Users Tab** - Roster of all users in this org - Columns: Name, Email, Role, Seats Assigned, Last Active - Actions: View, Edit Role, Revoke Access 5. **Activity Tab** - Full activity timeline - Filterable by type (license, user, billing, system) - Infinite scroll pagination 6. **Notes Tab** - Internal support notes - Add note form - Note history with timestamps **Right Sidebar (Associations)**: - Billing Account card (link to detail) - License Pools summary (count by status) - Recent Orders card - Support Tickets (if any) ### 4. Organization New/Edit (`admin/organizations/new.html`, `edit.html`) **Form Fields**: - Organization Name (required) - Type (select: School, District, Co-op) - Status (select: Active, Inactive) - Primary Contact - Name (required) - Email (required, validated) - Phone (optional) - Address (optional) - Street, City, State, Zip - Tags (multi-select) - Internal Notes (textarea) **Validation**: - Name: Required, unique check - Email: Valid format - Phone: Format hint (no strict validation) **Actions**: - Save (create/update) - Save & Add License (create, then redirect to license creation) - Cancel (return to list) ### 5. License Pools List (`admin/license-pools/index.html`) **Table Columns**: | Column | Sortable | Filterable | |--------|----------|------------| | Checkbox | - | - | | Pool ID | Yes | Yes (search) | | Organization | Yes | Yes (search) | | Plan | Yes | Yes (Base/Premium) | | Seats | Yes | - | | Utilization % | Yes | Yes (range) | | Expires | Yes | Yes (date range) | | Status | Yes | Yes (Active/Expiring/At-Risk/Expired) | | Actions | - | - | **Filters**: - Status: All, Active, Expiring Soon, At-Risk, Expired - Plan: All, Base, Premium - Utilization: All, Under 50%, 50-80%, Over 80% - Duration: All, 1-Year, 3-Year, 5-Year, Custom - Expires: Next 30 days, Next 90 days, This year, Custom range **Special Views**: - At-Risk Pools (`at-risk.html`) — Pre-filtered to status=at-risk - Expiring Soon — Pre-filtered to expires within 30 days ### 6. License Pool Detail (`admin/license-pools/detail.html`) **Left Sidebar**: - Pool ID - Plan badge (Base/Premium) - Duration badge - Status badge (color-coded) - Seat capacity (used/total) - Date range (start - end) - Days remaining - Funding type - Stripe Subscription ID (if applicable) **Center Column (Tabs)**: 1. **Seats Tab** - Table of all seat assignments - Columns: User, Email, Role, Assigned Date, Assigned By, Status - Actions: Reassign, Revoke - Empty state if no seats assigned 2. **Activity Tab** - Pool-specific activity log - Seat assignments, status changes, etc. 3. **Billing Tab** - Related invoices - Payment history **Right Sidebar**: - Organization card (link) - Billing Account card (link) - Other pools for same org ### 7. Users List (`admin/users/index.html`) **Table Columns**: - Checkbox - Name - Email - Organization - Role - Seats Assigned - Status - Last Active - Actions **Filters**: - Status: Active, Pending, Inactive - Role: Owner, Admin, Member - Organization: Search/select - Has Seat: Yes, No, All ### 8. User Detail (`admin/users/detail.html`) **Left Sidebar**: - Name (editable) - Email - Phone - Role badge - Status badge - Organization link - Last active timestamp **Center Column (Tabs)**: 1. **Seats Tab** — All seat assignments 2. **Activity Tab** — User activity log 3. **Notes Tab** — Internal notes **Right Sidebar**: - Organization card - Assigned licenses list ### 9. Impersonation Banner **Functionality**: When admin "impersonates" an organization: 1. State saved to LocalStorage 2. Banner appears at top of ALL pages: ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ⚠️ Viewing as: Springfield Elementary [End Impersonation] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` 3. Clicking "End Impersonation" clears state and returns to admin 4. Portal pages show the impersonated org's data --- ## Admin Layout Template All admin pages share: ```html
...
Dashboard Organizations License Pools Billing Users Orders Analytics Audit Logs Settings

Page Title

...
``` --- ## Deliverables 1. `admin/index.html` — Dashboard 2. `admin/organizations/index.html` — List 3. `admin/organizations/detail.html` — Detail (3-column) 4. `admin/organizations/new.html` — Create form 5. `admin/organizations/edit.html` — Edit form 6. `admin/license-pools/index.html` — List 7. `admin/license-pools/detail.html` — Detail (3-column) 8. `admin/license-pools/at-risk.html` — At-risk view 9. `admin/license-pools/create.html` — Manual creation form 10. `admin/users/index.html` — List 11. `admin/users/detail.html` — Detail 12. `admin/users/search.html` — User lookup 13. `shared/templates/partials/sidebar.admin.html` 14. `shared/templates/partials/header.html` 15. `shared/templates/partials/impersonation-banner.html` --- ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Dashboard loads with attention items and stats - [ ] Organizations list: sorting, filtering, pagination all work - [ ] Organization detail: all 6 tabs render content - [ ] License pools list: status filter correctly highlights at-risk - [ ] License pool detail: seats table shows assignments - [ ] Users list: search finds users by email - [ ] Impersonation: banner appears and persists across pages - [ ] All navigation links work (no 404s) - [ ] 3-column layout maintains proportions at 1280px+

description:
# Build License Pools List and Detail Pages

Create the license pools list page and detail page, reusing table and 3-column patterns from organizations.

## License Pools List (`admin/license-pools/index.html`)

### Table Columns
| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Checkbox | Bulk selection |
| Pool ID | Clickable link to detail |
| Organization | Link to org |
| Plan | Base or Premium badge |
| Duration | 1-year, 3-year, 5-year |
| Seats | Used / Total with progress bar |
| Status | Active, Expiring, At-Risk, Expired |
| Expires | Date with countdown |
| Actions | View, Edit, Renew |

### Filters
- Plan Type: All, Base, Premium
- Status: All, Active, Expiring Soon, At-Risk, Expired
- Duration: All, 1-Year, 3-Year, 5-Year
- Organization: Search/select
- Funding Type: All, Subscription, Grant, Purchase

### At-Risk View
Special filtered view (`at-risk.html`) showing only pools with:
- Status = at-risk OR
- Status = expiring AND days remaining < 30

## License Pool Detail (`admin/license-pools/detail.html`)

### Left Sidebar
- Pool ID
- Plan type badge (Base/Premium)
- Duration
- Start date / End date
- Days remaining (with color coding)
- Seat capacity (total)
- Status badge
- Funding type
- Source reference (PO number, grant name)
- Stripe subscription ID (if applicable)

### Center Tabs

**Seats Tab**
- Table of all seat assignments
- User name, email, assigned date, assigned by
- Actions: Revoke seat
- "Assign Seat" button

**Activity Tab**
- Timeline of pool events
- Created, renewed, seats assigned/revoked

**Billing Tab**
- Related invoices
- Payment history
- Next billing date (if subscription)

### Right Sidebar
- Organization card (linked)
- Billing Account card (linked)
- Related pools from same org

## Status Color Coding

| Status | Badge Color | Meaning |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| Active | Green | Healthy, >30 days remaining |
| Expiring | Yellow | 15-30 days remaining |
| At-Risk | Orange | Payment failed OR <15 days |
| Expired | Gray | Past end date |

## Verification

- [ ] List shows all pools from seed data
- [ ] Filters work correctly
- [ ] At-risk view filters automatically
- [ ] Detail shows correct pool data
- [ ] Seats tab shows assignments
- [ ] Related org/account links work
- [ ] Status badges color-coded correctly

- `src/admin/license-pools/detail.html`
- `src/admin/license-pools/at-risk.html`
- `src/admin/license-pools/create.html`

title: Build roster management with bulk import
description:
# Build Roster Management with Bulk Import

Create the roster management pages for the Organization Portal.

## Files to Create

- `portal/roster/index.html` - User list with filters, search, actions
- `portal/roster/invite.html` - Single user invite modal
- `portal/roster/bulk-import.html` - CSV upload wizard
- `portal/roster/pending.html` - Pending invitations list
- `portal/roster/user-detail.html` - User detail view

## Roster Index Page

**Table columns:** Name, Email, Role (Owner/Admin/Member), Seats assigned, Status, Last active, Actions

**Filters:** Role, Status (Active/Pending/Inactive), Has seat assigned

**Actions:** Edit, Assign seat, Resend invite, Remove

## Bulk Import Flow

1. **Upload:** Drag-drop or file picker (CSV only)
2. **Preview:** Show first 10 rows, detect columns
3. **Map columns:** Auto-map common headers (email, first_name, last_name)
4. **Validate:** Preflight check with error highlighting
5. **Import:** Progress bar
6. **Summary:** '42 created, 3 updated, 5 failed'

**Validation rules:**
- Required: email only (RFC 5322 compliant)
- Row limit: 500 per import
- Duplicate detection by email within org
- Error report as downloadable CSV

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Roster table shows all org users with correct data
- [ ] Filter by role/status works
- [ ] Invite modal validates email format
- [ ] Bulk import accepts CSV file
- [ ] Bulk import shows preview of first 10 rows
- [ ] Validation errors shown inline per row
- [ ] Failed rows downloadable as CSV
- [ ] Pending invites page shows resend button

## Verification

1. Load roster/index.html - table renders with mock data
2. Click 'Invite User' - modal opens with email input
3. Upload test CSV - preview appears
4. Submit invalid emails - error summary shows
5. Pending page shows invite with resend action

title: Document bulk import edge cases and validation
description:
# Bulk Import Edge Cases

## Purpose
Document and handle all edge cases for CSV roster import.

## Edge Cases to Handle

### File Issues
- [ ] Empty file → "File is empty"
- [ ] Too large (>5MB) → "File exceeds maximum size"
- [ ] Wrong encoding (not UTF-8) → Detect and warn
- [ ] Not a CSV → "Please upload a CSV file"
- [ ] BOM character at start → Strip automatically

### Data Issues
- [ ] Missing email column → "Email column required"
- [ ] Duplicate emails in file → "Rows 5, 12 have duplicate email"
- [ ] Invalid email format → "Row 7: Invalid email format"
- [ ] Email already in org → "Row 3: User already exists"
- [ ] Too many rows (>500) → "Maximum 500 rows per import"

### Column Mapping
- [ ] Unrecognized columns → Ignore with warning
- [ ] Multiple columns map to same field → Error
- [ ] Required field not mapped → Error

## Code is Functional WHEN...
- [ ] All edge cases show user-friendly error
- [ ] Partial success possible (skip bad rows)
- [ ] Error report downloadable as CSV
- [ ] No crashes or unhandled exceptions
acceptance_criteria: Edge cases documented (duplicate emails, malformed CSV). Validation rules specified. Error message templates defined. Example CSV format provided.
